anti_patterns.name: bare_except
anti_patterns.description: Bare 'except:' catches everything including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt. Always specify the exception type.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect except: without an exception class.
anti_patterns.fix: Use 'except Exception as e:' at minimum. Prefer catching specific exceptions.

anti_patterns.name: star_import
anti_patterns.description: Wildcard imports (from module import *) pollute the namespace and make it unclear where names come from.
anti_patterns.fix: Import specific names: from module import ClassA, function_b

anti_patterns.name: isinstance_chain
anti_patterns.description: Long chains of isinstance checks suggest missing polymorphism or pattern matching (match/case in 3.10+).
anti_patterns.detection: Detect 3+ consecutive isinstance checks in if/elif chains.
anti_patterns.fix: Use match/case (Python 3.10+), dictionary dispatch, or polymorphism.

anti_patterns.name: global_variable
anti_patterns.description: Global mutable state makes code hard to test and reason about. Pass state explicitly or use dependency injection.
anti_patterns.fix: Pass the value as a function parameter. Use a class to encapsulate state.

anti_patterns.name: string_format_percent
anti_patterns.description: % string formatting is outdated. Use f-strings (3.6+) or str.format() for clarity and safety.
